assertions.expectation: Explains that GKE's maximum upgrade parallelism is approximately 20 nodes simultaneously, regardless of maxSurge setting
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'GKE's maximum upgrade parallelism is ~20 nodes simultaneously regardless of maxSurge settings'

assertions.expectation: Calculates that at 20-node parallelism, upgrading 3,000 nodes requires ~150 batches — far more than an 8-hour window allows
assertions.reason: Response provides calculation: '3,000 nodes ÷ 20 nodes/batch × ~5-10 min/batch = 12.5-25 hours minimum upgrade time' and shows ~150 batches

assertions.expectation: Recommends splitting upgrades across multiple maintenance windows (weekends) rather than attempting all at once
assertions.reason: Response explicitly recommends 'Staggered multi-weekend approach' with Weekend 1 for CPU pools and Weekend 2 for GPU pools

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading CPU pools and GPU pools separately, prioritizing CPU pools first as they're less disruptive
assertions.reason: Response recommends 'Weekend 1: CPU pools only' and explains 'CPU pools upgrade faster than GPU pools (no driver concerns, typically more surge capacity available)'

assertions.expectation: Recommends upgrading GPU pools during scheduled gaps between training/inference workloads
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Training job protection strategy' with maintenance exclusions and timing considerations for long-running training jobs

assertions.expectation: Suggests expanding the maintenance window or using multiple consecutive windows for large pool upgrades
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Maintenance window expansion' section with specific commands to extend to 16+ hours and Friday night to Sunday morning options

assertions.expectation: Mentions that cluster autoscaler can only scale one node pool at a time — this interacts with upgrade scheduling
assertions.reason: Response does not mention cluster autoscaler limitations or how it interacts with upgrade scheduling

assertions.expectation: Addresses the 15,000 node standard limit and notes GKE 1.31+ supports up to 65,000 for AI workloads
assertions.reason: Response does not mention the 15,000 node standard limit or the 65,000 node limit for AI workloads in GKE 1.31+
